session_note: Warm session (store had a prior April-14 conversation handled by a HUMAN agent 'Krista' that auto-closed). T1-T3 latency contaminated by warm-history load + detector tuning; clean latencies are T4-T10.

turns.q: Hi! I'm totally new to press-on nails and my natural nails are weak and short. Which of your sets last the longest and won't damage my nails?
turns.a: For longest wear use standard glue-on press-ons (last up to 2 weeks) rather than Quick Press Mani. For weak/short nails the gentlest option is nail tabs instead of glue (easier removal, ideal for delicate nail beds). Start with a super short / short shape. Beginner-friendly picks: Think Pink, Pinky Promise, Short & Suite.

turns.q: I love a natural, neutral look — nothing too long or flashy. Between those, which is the most natural and truly beginner-friendly?
turns.a: Ma Damn is the most natural and beginner-friendly: classic short almond shape, semi-transparent natural base with white French tip — polished without feeling flashy.

turns.q: How long does Ma Damn actually last, and how do I apply and remove it safely so I don't wreck my weak natural nails?
turns.a: Up to 2 weeks with proper application/removal. Safe application: small amount of glue, avoid skin, press 10s; prep by pushing back cuticles, lightly buffing, cleaning nails. Removal: soak in warm water + oil + soap 10–15 min, lift from sides, never pry; buff leftover glue, moisturize.

turns.q: My nails are quite small and narrow. How do I make sure Ma Damn fits, and what happens if a size doesn't fit me?
turns.a: Short Almond shape ranges ~6.3mm–14.01mm wide — works for small/narrow nails. 24 nails in 12 sizes, size number on the back, file sides for custom fit. Test each nail before applying (sidewall to sidewall, no skin overlap); size down or file if too wide, size up if too narrow. Can send a photo of bare nails for fit help.

turns.q: Do I need anything else to apply them safely as a beginner — like a prep kit, the right glue, or those nail tabs you mentioned?
turns.a: Set already includes glue, alcohol pad, file, cuticle stick. Best add-on for weak nails: Adhesive Nail Tabs (gentler, easier removal, good for a wear test). Brush-On Nail Glue = easiest application. Best one-stop beginner setup: the Pressed to Perfection bundle (Brush-On Glue + Adhesive Nail Tabs).

turns.q: If I get Ma Damn plus the Pressed to Perfection bundle, what's my total, and is there a first-order discount for new customers?
turns.a: Ma Damn $15.99 + Pressed to Perfection $16.18 = $32.17 before tax/shipping. New-customer 20% off offer exists (link). No first-order code confirmed in chat and no automatic discounts applying right now.

turns.q: How fast is shipping to the US, and do I get free shipping if I add both items?
turns.a: "I don't have the US shipping timing or free shipping threshold confirmed here yet." — deflects back to product questions.

turns.q: Okay, I'm sold. Can you add Ma Damn and the Pressed to Perfection bundle to my cart?
turns.a: Can't add to cart directly, but surfaces the product cards (add from each page) + quick-reply chips: Check product details / Need help with checkout? / More product options.

turns.q: I also wear lashes. Do you have a magnetic lash that would match this soft natural look for a full set?
turns.a: Virgo is the most natural-looking magnetic lash, good for everyday. Needs a magnetic liner (sold separately). Offers liner options as chips: Natural Look / Classic Black Liner / Hybrid Liner / More Options.

turns.q: Let's go with the classic black liner. What's the best way to check out, and can I use that 20% new-customer offer at checkout?
turns.a: Open each product page from the cards, add to cart, checkout from cart. 20% new-customer offer link provided. Best classic black liner: Magnetic Eyeliner Pen in Black $27.99 (in stock). No automatic discounts right now. Offers to line up the full set.
